PER CCP SECTION 1032(A)(4), PLAINTIFF THRIVENT FINANCIAL FOR LUTHERANS IS THE PREVAILING PARTY SINCE IT OBTAINED A JUDGMENT FAVORABLE TO IT ON THE TWO ASSERTED REGULATIONS THAT WERE THE SUBJECT OF THIS CASE. ALL OF THRIVENT'S CLAIMED COSTS ARE EITHER ALLOWABLE AS A MATTER OF RIGHT OR IN THE COURT'S DISCRETION AND, AS TO THOSE CLAIMED COSTS THAT ARE PERMITTED IN THE EXERCISE OF THE COURT'S DISCRETION, THE COURT EXERCISES ITS DISCRETION TO ALLOW ALL SUCH COSTS. ALL OF THE COSTS CLAIMED BY THRIVENT WERE REASONABLY NECESSARY FOR THIS CASE AND ARE REASONABLE IN AMOUNT. WHILE THE COURT AT TIMES QUESTIONED THE PARTIES REGARDING WHAT WAS NECESSARY FOR THE RESOLUTION OF THIS CASE, LOOKING NOW IN HINDSIGHT, THE COURT DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT ANY OF THE COSTS INCURRED BY THRIVENT WERE UNREASONABLY INCURRED.ORDER IS SIGNED. LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF THRIVENT FINANCIAL FOR LUTHERANS' MOTION TO SEAL CERTAIN DOCUMENTS.PLAINTIFF THRIVENT FINANCIAL FOR LUTHERANS' MOTION TO SEAL CERTAIN DOCUMENTS IS DENIED. THE MOTION TO SEAL PAPERS AND THE DECLARATION SOUGHT TO BE SEALED FAIL TO MAKE THE REQUIRED "SPECIFIC SHOWING OF SERIOUS INJURY" IF THE DECLARATION SOUGHT TO BE SEALED IS MADE AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC. (UNIVERSAL CITY STUDIOS, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (2003) 110 CAL. APP. 4TH 1273, 1282). "IN DELINEATING THE INJURY TO BE PREVENTED, SPECIFICITY IS ESSENTIAL. BROAD ALLEGATIONS OF HARM, BEREFT OF SPECIFIC EXAMPLES OF ARTICULATED REASONING, ARE INSUFFICIENT." (ID. QUOTING IN RE CENDENT (3RD CIR. 2001) 260 F.3D 183, 194). MOREOVER, BECAUSE THE DECLARATION SOUGHT TO BE SEALED IS OFFERED TO SHOW THAT A SIGNIFICANT BENEFIT HAS BEEN CONFERRED ON A LARGE CLASS OF PERSONS IN ORDER TO OBTAIN AN ORDER REQUIRING A GOVERNMENTAL AGENCY TO PAY A SUBSTANTIAL AMOUNT OF FEES, THERE IS A HEIGHTENED RIGHT OF PUBLIC ACCESS FOR ALL MATERIALS ON WHICH THE COURT MIGHT RELY IN RULING ON THE MOTION. DUE TO THE CONCERNS EXPRESSED BY THE DECLARANT OF THE DECLARATION SOUGHT TO BE SEALED, THE COURT IS AMENABLE TO THRIVENT'S WITHDRAWING THE DECLARATION RATHER THAN HAVING IT MADE AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC AND, IN SUCH EVENT, THE COURT'S RULING ON THE MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION WOULD NOT INCLUDE ANY REFERENCE TO THE DECLARATION. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE ORDER ENTERED ON JANUARY 19, 2019. (SPECIALLY SET AT 9:00 AM BEFORE JUDGE KAHN)PLAINTIFF THRIVENT FINANCIAL FOR LUTHERANS' MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE ORDER DENYING ITS MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES IS DENIED. ASSUMING WITHOUT DECIDING THAT THE MOTION IS PERMITTED BY CCP 1008 OR, IF NOT, IS A PROPER INVITATION FOR THE COURT TO EXERCISE ITS AUTHORITY PER LE FRANCOIS V. GOEL (2005) 35 CAL. 4TH 1094 TO RECONSIDER THE PRIOR ORDER WHICH THE COURT WOULD ACCEPT, THRIVENT HAS FAILED TO SHOW THAT THE JUDGMENT INVALIDATING THE "UNDERGROUND" REGULATIONS CONFERRED A SIGNIFICANT BENEFIT ON THE GENERAL PUBLIC OR A LARGE CLASS OF PERSONS. THE SALIENT PORTIONS OF THE WESTMARK DECLARATION ARE INADMISSIBLE HEARSAY. THE SALIENT PORTIONS OF THE LOVENDUSKY DECLARATION LACK FOUNDATION, ARE CONCLUSIONS OF LAW AND/OR ARE SPECULATION. THE SALIENT PORTIONS OR THE DECLARATION SOUGHT TO BE SEALED, EVEN IF CONSIDERED, FAIL TO SHOW THAT THE DECLARANT'S THREE COMPANIES RECEIVED A SIGNIFICANT BENEFIT FROM THE JUDGMENT IN THIS CASE BECAUSE OF THE PRACTICES THAT THOSE COMPANIES HAD IN PLACE BEFORE THE JUDGMENT AND, EVEN IF THOSE COMPANIES DID RECEIVE A SIGNIFICANT BENEFIT FROM THE JUDGMENT, THE THREE COMPANIES DO NOT CONSTITUTE A "LARGE CLASS OF PERSONS" WITHIN THE MEANING OF CCP 1021.5.COUNSEL FOR THE CONTROLLER IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Certain Documents; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar May-06-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF THRIVENT FINANCIAL FOR LUTHERANS' Motion To Seal Certain Documents.

Plaintiff Thrivent Financial for Lutherans' motion to seal certain documents is denied. The motion to seal papers and the declaration sought to be sealed fail to make the required "specific showing of serious injury" if the declaration sought to be sealed is made available to the public. (Universal City Studios, Inc. v. Superior Court (2003) 110 Cal. App. 4th 1273, 1282). "In delineating the injury to be prevented, specificity is essential. Broad allegations of harm, bereft of specific examples of articulated reasoning, are insufficient." (Id. quoting In re Cendent (3rd Cir. 2001) 260 F.3d 183, 194). Moreover, because the declaration sought to be sealed is offered to show that a significant benefit has been conferred on a large class of persons in order to obtain an order requiring a governmental agency to pay a substantial amount of fees, there is a heightened right of public access for all materials on which the court might rely in ruling on the motion. Due to the concerns expressed by the declarant of the declaration sought to be sealed, the court is amenable to Thrivent's withdrawing the declaration rather than having it made available to the public and, in such event, the court's ruling on the motion for reconsideration would not include any reference to the declaration.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/HK)

Plaintiff'S Motion For Reconsideration Of The Order Entered On January 19, 2019. (Specially Set At 9:00 Am Before Judge Kahn)
Off Calendar May-06-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff'S Motion For Reconsideration Of The Order Entered On January 19, 2019. (Specially Set At 9:00 Am Before Judge Kahn)

Plaintiff Thrivent Financial for Lutherans' motion for reconsideration of the order denying its motion for attorneys' fees is denied. Assuming without deciding that the motion is permitted by CCP 1008 or, if not, is a proper invitation for the court to exercise its authority per Le Francois v. Goel (2005) 35 Cal. 4th 1094 to reconsider the prior order which the court would accept, Thrivent has failed to show that the judgment invalidating the "underground" regulations conferred a significant benefit on the general public or a large class of persons. The salient portions of the Westmark declaration are inadmissible hearsay. The salient portions of the Lovendusky declaration lack foundation, are conclusions of law and/or are speculation. The salient portions or the declaration sought to be sealed, even if considered, fail to show that the declarant's three companies received a significant benefit from the judgment in this case because of the practices that those companies had in place before the judgment and, even if those companies did receive a significant benefit from the judgment, the three companies do not constitute a "large class of persons" within the meaning of CCP 1021.5.

Counsel for the Controller is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/HK)

APPEARANCES: Atty. ANDREW B. KAY, ESQ. of Cozen O'Connor (Tel: 202-912-4864) and atty. ANDREW D. LANPHERE, ESQ. of Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP (Tel: 415-983-1321) appearing for plaintiff Thrivent Financial for Lutherans;

The matter is argued and submitted by counsel for both sides. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Thrivent Financial for Lutherans' motion for attorneys' fees is denied. Even considering all of the evidence submitted with the reply papers, Thrivent has not shown that the judgment in its favor conferred a significant benefit on the general public or a large class of persons, which is a required element for the award of attorneys' fees per CCP 1021.5. Specifically, Thrivent has not shown that any life insurer other than it or any insureds or beneficiaries of life insurance policies issued by another life insurer has benefitted in any significant way by the judgment issued in this case.

Counsel for the State Controller are to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Alicia Clough, Esq. and Marc Cohen, Esq. 310-282-2000 of Loeb & Loeb for Defendant OFFICE OF THE STATE CONTROLLER, BETTY YEE.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court raises two issues re: prevailing party and reasonableness of costs. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT THE OFFICE OF THE STATE CONTROLLER, BETTY YEE's Motion To Strike Thrivent'S Memorandum Of Costs Or, In The Alternative, To Tax Costs Claimed By Thrivent.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants Betty Yee and the Office of the State Controller's motion to strike Thrivent memorandum of costs and alternative motion to tax costs claimed by Thrivent are denied in their entirety. Per CCP section 1032(a)(4), plaintiff Thrivent Financial for Lutherans is the prevailing party since it obtained a judgment favorable to it on the two asserted regulations that were the subject of this case. All of Thrivent's claimed costs are either allowable as a matter of right or in the court's discretion and, as to those claimed costs that are permitted in the exercise of the court's discretion, the court exercises its discretion to allow all such costs. All of the costs claimed by Thrivent were reasonably necessary for this case and are reasonable in amount. While the court at times questioned the parties regarding what was necessary for the resolution of this case, looking now in hindsight, the court does not believe that any of the costs incurred by Thrivent were unreasonably incurred.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
